{"parentUuid":"cb9837b8-91ee-491c-a721-f38370d9bfb3","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"2dd4727c-ea1e-4329-9b10-a025b945bf6e","agentId":"ad8b14e39b6cb2d21","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019amooL2z5i1EPXpm2PM8RZ","type":"tool_result","content":"73	## §3 Design Pillars + tokens
74	
75	These are cross-cutting principles every WP in v3 references. Synthesised from R-A whitespace + cursor language, R-B AI-philosophy posture, R-G token-system audit, and R-D's anti-pattern catalog (the **negative reference**).
76	
77	### Pillars
78	
79	| ID  | Pillar                                                | One-line                                                                                                                       |
80	| --- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
81	| D1  | **Direct manipulation first, panels second**          | If a property has a visible representation, you should be able to drag it. Inspector is the secondary path, not primary.       |
82	| D2  | **Live preview always**                                | No drag, no input, no command should ever be type-and-pray. 60 fps preview before commit.                                       |
83	| D3  | **Density-conscious, but never crowded**               | Small typography, tight spacing, but every glyph carries meaning. No decorative chrome.                                        |
84	| D4  | **Color is information, not decoration**               | Palette serves discipline tints, advisory severity, drift, and category — never branding flourish.                              |
85	| D5  | **Motion conveys causality**                           | Every state change has an 80–240 ms motion arc that explains what happened. No teleporting elements.                              |
86	| D6  | **Comment & markup is first-class, not bolt-on**       | Anchor any comment to any element / region / sheet at any zoom level. Threadable. Resolvable. Never modal.                       |
87	| D7  | **The activity stream is the time machine**            | Every change is rewindable + restorable. Undo is the keyboard shortcut; the activity stream is the visual surface.               |
88	| D8  | **Context, not configuration**                         | Tools change behavior based on what's selected, what's hovered, what view is active. Less mode-switching, more sensing.            |
89	| D9  | **Mobile is parallel, not nested**                     | iPad sketch + viewer is its own surface — not a "responsive" version of desktop. Different jobs.                                  |
90	| D10 | **Output is a feature, not an afterthought**           | PDF / PPTX / web export is in the same code-paths as the canvas, not a separate exporter team. Brand templates are first-class.   |
91	
92	### Anti-pattern catalog (negative reference, from R-D §5)
93	
94	| Anti-pattern (R-D)                          | Pillar violated     | v3 antidote                                                          |
95	| ------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
96	| A1 Modal sketch sessions                     | D8                  | Soft contextual scopes (already shipped via SKT-01)                    |
97	| A2 Type vs Instance dichotomy in dialog      | D1                  | Single Inspector with "applies to: this / all 12" radio                 |
98	| A3 Ribbon-with-30-tabs                       | D3                  | Cmd palette + contextual panel                                          |
99	| A4 Dialog-for-everything                     | D1                  | Inspector + drawer overlay                                              |
100	| A5 Tooltip-as-documentation                   | D3                  | Text labels at panel widths ≥ 240 px                                    |
101	| A6 Worksets / central-file vocab              | D7                  | Figma-grade vocab; activity stream as time machine                       |
102	| A7 Phasing dual-dropdown                     | D8                  | Single "view shows: existing / demolition / new" dropdown bound to view  |
103	| A8 VG matrix dialog (~120 toggles)           | D8                  | Discipline lens + view template + view filter + right-click on a category |
104	| A9 Always-visible Properties panel           | D1                  | Inspector appears only on selection; empty-state is the model itself     |
105	| A10 "Project Browser blue" engineering palette | D4                | Warm ochre accent + drafting palette + R-G phased adoption                |
106	
107	### Token reference (full proposal in `spec/v3-research/design-tokens.md`)
108	
109	The v3 token set is 3-layer-separated:
110	
111	- **Layer A — Structural (always ours).** `--space-*`, `--radius-*`, `--text-*`, `--motion-*`, `--ease-*`, `--draft-*`, `--cat-*`, `--disc-*`, `--color-{success,warning,danger,info,drift}`. Never themed.
112	- **Layer B — Semantic Shell (light / dark / customer).** `--color-{background,foreground,surface*,border*,muted-foreground,ring}`. Swap between light, dark, and customer-tinted variants without touching brand.
113	- **Layer C — Brand (re-themable to customer's CI).** `--brand-accent`, `--brand-accent-fg`, `--brand-logo-mark`, `--brand-typeface`. A customer's CI is a single CSS file. **Brand-swap CI invariant test** asserts only Layer C resolves change when the brand is swapped — drafting palette, line weights, motion stay byte-identical.
114	
115	**Headline token shifts in v3:**
116	
117	- Chrome accent `hsl(214 88% 50%)` cobalt → `#b8763a` warm ochre (light) / `#d9924a` (dark). The single biggest "feels engineering" tell, replaced.
118	- Discipline tints: `--disc-arch` sage `#6e8a72`, `--disc-struct` taupe `#8b6f57`, `--disc-mep` slate-blue `#5b7a8c`. Each with a `-soft` variant via `color-mix`. Feeds T8.
119	- New `--color-drift` (light `#9b6f1b` / dark `#d2a256`) — first-class severity, separates drift from warning.
120	- New `--text-2xs` (10 px / 14 px) — legitimises the canvas-overlay regime where 627 `text-[Npx]` escapes live today.
121	- New `--ease-paper` cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) — slow-finish for commit/settle moments. Reads as ink hitting paper.
122	- New `--radius-canvas: 0` — sidebars, rails, status bar, plan / 3D / sheet canvases never get radius. Codifies the "sidebars are sidebars, not cards" rule.
123	
124	Adoption is phased: introduce `tokens-v3.css` alongside v1 (no component changes), then migrate by feature folder (workspace → plan → viewport → families → tools), then deprecate hex literals via an ESLint rule.
125	
126	### Rendering-quality opinions (canvas-side, the marquee surface)
127	
128	**Plan canvas (the single biggest design-quality lever):**
129	
130	- Cut lines heavy, projection fine, witness hairline. Hatches scale with paper-mm at the active plot scale, not screen pixels. Dimension typography in tabular nums at `--text-2xs`. Witness lines `--draft-witness` at `--draft-lw-witness`. The padlock glyph from R-A (associative-snap acquired) is implemented as a 12 × 12 px overlay near the cursor when `snapEngine` returns an associative match.
131	- Line weights at zoom levels: at 1:50 plot scale, cut lines are 2 px (major) / 1.4 px (minor); at 1:100, 70 %; at 1:200, 50 %; at 1:500, 0.4 px cut + projection suppressed.
132	- Grid: major at zoom > 1:200 only; minor at zoom > 1:100 only.
133	
134	**3D viewport:**
135	
136	- Background is a linear vertical gradient, `--draft-paper` at horizon to slightly darker at top. Avoid pure white (studio-photo) and pure grey (engineering-drab).
137	- Sun at 35° elevation, 145° azimuth — north-east-by-east, a real architectural sun position.
138	- Ochre selection halo at 2 px stroke + 0.4 opacity glow. Never blue. Never a thick rectangle.
139	- SSAO tuned toward "drawn shadow," not "rendered shadow" — short occlusion radius, gentle falloff.
140	- Paper-grain noise on wall / floor materials at low intensity so surfaces don't read as plastic.
141	
142	These canvas decisions are tracked as WPs inside T2 and T4. Token tables alone don't make a product feel architectural — canvas rendering with token-driven discipline does.
143	
144	---
145	
146	## §UX Workflow Design — chrome layout, top bar, role-based workspaces, tool grammar
147	
148	### Why this section
149	
150	The nine themes describe **what bim-ai does**; this section describes **the chrome it does it inside** — top bar, side rails, status bar, command grammar, workspace switcher. Revit's chrome is the counter-example: a 12-tab ribbon (A3), an always-visible 320 px Properties panel (A9), a 120-toggle VG matrix dialog (A8), icon-only buttons that depend on tooltips for documentation (A5), and a "Project Browser blue" palette (A10). Every one of those is a chrome decision; every one is what we are not building.
151	
152	The bet is that **good chrome is invisible**. Where Revit asks "what dialog do I open?" bim-ai answers "the rails appear on selection; the dropdown lives where you'd expect; `Cmd+K` covers everything else." T1's phasing dropdown lives in the view header, T3's activity stream behind the top-bar entry, T8's lens in the status bar, T9's agent panel slides in from the right. Get the chrome right and themes inherit; get it wrong and every theme fights the chassis.

162	│  [W] Wall   Align: ◐ Center ▾   Chain ☑   Multiple ☐   Loop (L)   …      │"}]},"uuid":"294c5b18-8576-4dac-8786-cce2ab0a9d5c","timestamp":"2026-05-08T13:24:47.001Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"cb9837b8-91ee-491c-a721-f38370d9bfb3","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai","sessionId":"aff6462c-5f6a-48fd-8e44-f7297e3332f0","version":"2.1.133","gitBranch":"main"}

414	### Section takeaway
415	
416	Chrome that gets out of the way is the second-largest design-quality lever after canvas rendering quality. A top bar that holds identity not tools, a status bar that holds the lens not a 120-toggle dialog, an Inspector that appears only on selection, a `Cmd+K` palette that absorbs every long-tail action, a tool grammar uniform across every authoring verb, and discipline-tinted chrome that signals workspace at a glance — together these make a young architect say "this feels architectural, not engineering" in the first thirty seconds. **Chrome and canvas are 90 % of the "feels architectural" bet.**

3130:**Engine.** New module `app/bim_ai/tkn/` with `encode(elements) → TokenSequence` (deterministic order), `decode(seq, kernel_state) → list[Command]`, `validate(seq) → list[Advisory]`, `diff(a, b) → TokenSequenceDelta`. Wired into `try_commit_bundle`: every bundle round-trips through `encode → decode → encode` and produces a deep-equal sequence (modulo float epsilon). CLI: `bim-ai apply-bundle <bundle.json> --base <revision> [--dry-run | --commit]`. REST: `POST /api/v3/bundles`. Both registered per API-V3-01. Idempotent: same bundle replayed against the same parent yields the same new revision.

The brief is passed through as metadata only — the deterministic CV does not interpret it; agents that read the brief do their own thing with it.

1233:**Why it matters.** T3 is the wedge for **B3 Live Collaboration with Time Machine** — the Figma-meets-Bluebeam bet. Revit's collaboration vocabulary (worksets, central files, "synchronize with central") is Subversion-era and reads as A6 in our anti-pattern catalog (R-D §5). Motif's deepest pillar today is collaboration semantics, not authoring (R-B §6) — Motif is "unambiguously deeper" than Rayon there, and that is the area where v3-T3 should learn most from Motif. Closing T3 unlocks the whole B3 felt-outcome — two architects co-author the model live, a third reviewer leaves twelve 3D-anchored comments on a sheet, and the activity stream is a real time machine. T3 is also the surface that pays back **D6 Comment & markup is first-class** and **D7 Activity stream is the time machine** — the two pillars that distinguish v3 from every Revit-clone in the market. Without T3, B3 is unbuildable; without B3, the small-practice studio that picked bim-ai for B1 still has to ship reviews via marked-up PDFs and email. **CQ-01 (WebSocket robustness) is a hard prerequisite** — unreliable WS = unreliable multi-user; the v3 tracker §7 calls this out explicitly. T3's distinctive vibe is **async-first + spatially-anchored**, deliberately not live-cursor-voice (which lives in V-06).
1273:**Scope.** **Hard prerequisite: CQ-01 WebSocket robustness must complete before this WP ships** — unreliable WS = silent state divergence in multi-user sessions; the v3 tracker §7 calls this out explicitly. Pick **yjs** (CRDT) over WS+OT for v3 — yjs is the production-grade web CRDT, integrates with Y-WebSocket on the server, supports awareness (presence) as a side-channel, and naturally extends to offline-tolerant authoring (COL-V3-06). The v2 architecture is server-authoritative on commit with broadcast via websocket and per-user undo stacks (per §O OpenBIM Stance) — yjs slots in as the in-flight transport between commits without changing the snapshot model. Each kernel command becomes a yjs Y.Doc transaction; the server is still the authority that resolves the canonical snapshot at every commit boundary.

# v3 Build State

Last updated: 2026-05-08 (bootstrap)
Active heavy workers: 0 / 8

## Live workers

| NNN | WP-ID | Prompt | Branch | Status | Started | Reported |
| --- | ----- | ------ | ------ | ------ | ------- | -------- |

| 002 | EDT-V3-01 constraint rules (parallel / perpendicular / collinear / equal_length) | wp-002.md | feat/v3-edt-v3-01-constraint-rules | dispatched-pending-prompt | 2026-05-08 | — |
| 003 | API-V3-01 tool/CLI surface contract | wp-003.md | feat/v3-api-v3-01-tool-cli-surface | dispatched-pending-prompt | 2026-05-08 | — |
| 004 | KRN-V3-01 phasing primitive | wp-004.md | feat/v3-krn-v3-01-phasing | dispatched-pending-prompt | 2026-05-08 | — |
| 005 | TKN-V3-01 tokenised kernel representation | wp-005.md | feat/v3-tkn-v3-01-tokenisation | dispatched-pending-prompt | 2026-05-08 | — |
| 006 | KRN-V3-06 plan region | wp-006.md | feat/v3-krn-v3-06-plan-region | dispatched-pending-prompt | 2026-05-08 | — |
| 007 | SUN-V3-01 sun & shadow study | wp-007.md | feat/v3-sun-v3-01-sun-shadow | dispatched-pending-prompt | 2026-05-08 | — |
| 008 | CQ-02 Python dependency pinning + lockfile | wp-008.md | feat/v3-cq-02-python-lockfile | dispatched-pending-prompt | 2026-05-08 | — |

Statuses: `dispatched-pending-prompt` (writer subagent in flight), `dispatched` (prompt file written, awaiting user to start worker), `working` (worker started, no report yet), `pushed` (branch pushed, awaiting review-subagent), `reviewed-pass` / `reviewed-fail`, `merged`, `failed`.

## Merged so far

| WP-ID | Branch | Merged at | Tracker status |
| ----- | ------ | --------- | -------------- |
| (none yet) | | | |

## Held back (deps not ready)

- **KRN-V3-02 stacked walls** — heavy `engine.py` overlap with KRN-V3-01 (WP-004); dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- **KRN-V3-03 multi-roof + fascia/gutter + soffit** — heavy `engine.py` overlap with KRN-V3-01; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- **KRN-V3-05 stair-by-sketch** — `engine.py` overlap; needs clean kernel after KRN-V3-01 lands.
- **CMD-V3-01 command-bundle apply** — depends on API-V3-01 (WP-003) + TKN-V3-01 (WP-005); dispatch after both merge.
- **CQ-01 WebSocket robustness** — non-trivial; schedule after Sprint 0 lands (WP-001 unblocks momentum).
- **CQ-04 god-file split** — high merge-conflict risk against ANY in-flight T1/T9 work; schedule for quiet window between bet-cycles.
- **CQ-03 workspace/ reorg** — riskiest CQ item; dedicated quiet week needed; WSP-V3-01 (T8) has soft prereq.
- **All CHR-V3-* chrome WPs** — wait for Sprint 0 (WP-001) merge so they inherit v3 tokens automatically.
- **COL-V3-01 multi-user** — hard deps: CQ-01 must land first.

## File-overlap notes

- WP-004 (KRN-V3-01 phasing) and WP-006 (KRN-V3-06 plan region) both touch `app/bim_ai/engine.py` in different sub-areas (phasing vs view_range). Low conflict risk but watch on merge — dispatch WP-006 only after confirming WP-004's exact engine.py changes.
- KRN-V3-02 stacked walls + KRN-V3-03 multi-roof held back to avoid triple `engine.py` overlap with WP-004; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- WP-003 (API-V3-01) touches `packages/cli/cli.mjs` + new `app/bim_ai/api/` (mostly new). Low overlap with others.
- WP-005 (TKN-V3-01) is mostly green-field in new `app/bim_ai/tkn/` module; thin `engine.py` integration.
- WP-007 (SUN-V3-01) touches `packages/web/src/viewport/` + small `engine.py` (SunSettings). Disjoint from WP-004/006 sub-areas.
- WP-008 (CQ-02) touches `app/pyproject.toml` + new `app/uv.lock` + `Makefile` + CI yaml. Zero overlap with everything else.
- CQ-04 god-file split is a known merge-conflict magnet vs every in-flight T1 / T9 WP; schedule between bet-cycles only.

## Notes

- Sprint 0 (WP-001) MUST merge before any CHR-V3-* or new-component WP starts.
- CQ-01 is a hard prereq for COL-V3-01 (T3) + JOB-V3-01 + OUT-V3-01.
- API-V3-01 (WP-003) cross-cuts every kernel WP per §U — every WP that lands a new kernel verb must add CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log per the API-V3-01 contract once that WP is merged.
- TKN-V3-01 (WP-005) is a gating keystone for CMD-V3-01, OPT-V3-01, IMG-V3-01 — dispatch those only after WP-005 merges.
- Concurrency cap: 8 heavy workers in flight at any time.
- WP-004 and WP-005 are both XL/L effort — they will be in flight simultaneously. Watch for `engine.py` conflicts if both are active; they touch different sub-areas.

- **KRN-V3-02 stacked walls** — heavy `engine.py` overlap with KRN-V3-01 (WP-004); dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- **KRN-V3-03 multi-roof + fascia/gutter + soffit** — heavy `engine.py` overlap with KRN-V3-01; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- **KRN-V3-05 stair-by-sketch** — `engine.py` overlap; needs clean kernel after KRN-V3-01 lands.
- **CMD-V3-01 command-bundle apply** — depends on API-V3-01 (WP-003) + TKN-V3-01 (WP-005); dispatch after both merge.
- **CQ-01 WebSocket robustness** — non-trivial; schedule after Sprint 0 lands (WP-001 unblocks momentum).

- **COL-V3-01 multi-user** — hard deps: CQ-01 must land first.

## File-overlap notes

- WP-004 (KRN-V3-01 phasing) and WP-006 (KRN-V3-06 plan region) both touch `app/bim_ai/engine.py` in different sub-areas (phasing vs view_range). Low conflict risk but watch on merge — dispatch WP-006 only after confirming WP-004's exact engine.py changes.
- KRN-V3-02 stacked walls + KRN-V3-03 multi-roof held back to avoid triple `engine.py` overlap with WP-004; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- WP-003 (API-V3-01) touches `packages/cli/cli.mjs` + new `app/bim_ai/api/` (mostly new). Low overlap with others.
- WP-005 (TKN-V3-01) is mostly green-field in new `app/bim_ai/tkn/` module; thin `engine.py` integration.
- WP-007 (SUN-V3-01) touches `packages/web/src/viewport/` + small `engine.py` (SunSettings). Disjoint from WP-004/006 sub-areas.
- WP-008 (CQ-02) touches `app/pyproject.toml` + new `app/uv.lock` + `Makefile` + CI yaml. Zero overlap with everything else.
- CQ-04 god-file split is a known merge-conflict magnet vs every in-flight T1 / T9 WP; schedule between bet-cycles only.

## Notes

- Sprint 0 (WP-001) MUST merge before any CHR-V3-* or new-component WP starts.
- CQ-01 is a hard prereq for COL-V3-01 (T3) + JOB-V3-01 + OUT-V3-01.
- API-V3-01 (WP-003) cross-cuts every kernel WP per §U — every WP that lands a new kernel verb must add CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log per the API-V3-01 contract once that WP is merged.
- TKN-V3-01 (WP-005) is a gating keystone for CMD-V3-01, OPT-V3-01, IMG-V3-01 — dispatch those only after WP-005 merges.
- Concurrency cap: 8 heavy workers in flight at any time.
- WP-004 and WP-005 are both XL/L effort — they will be in flight simultaneously. Watch for `engine.py` conflicts if both are active; they touch different sub-areas.

- WP-004 (KRN-V3-01 phasing) and WP-006 (KRN-V3-06 plan region) both touch `app/bim_ai/engine.py` in different sub-areas (phasing vs view_range). Low conflict risk but watch on merge — dispatch WP-006 only after confirming WP-004's exact engine.py changes.
- KRN-V3-02 stacked walls + KRN-V3-03 multi-roof held back to avoid triple `engine.py` overlap with WP-004; dispatch after WP-004 merges.
- WP-003 (API-V3-01) touches `packages/cli/cli.mjs` + new `app/bim_ai/api/` (mostly new). Low overlap with others.
- WP-005 (TKN-V3-01) is mostly green-field in new `app/bim_ai/tkn/` module; thin `engine.py` integration.
- WP-007 (SUN-V3-01) touches `packages/web/src/viewport/` + small `engine.py` (SunSettings). Disjoint from WP-004/006 sub-areas.
- WP-008 (CQ-02) touches `app/pyproject.toml` + new `app/uv.lock` + `Makefile` + CI yaml. Zero overlap with everything else.
- CQ-04 god-file split is a known merge-conflict magnet vs every in-flight T1 / T9 WP; schedule between bet-cycles only.

## Notes

- Sprint 0 (WP-001) MUST merge before any CHR-V3-* or new-component WP starts.
- CQ-01 is a hard prereq for COL-V3-01 (T3) + JOB-V3-01 + OUT-V3-01.
- API-V3-01 (WP-003) cross-cuts every kernel WP per §U — every WP that lands a new kernel verb must add CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log per the API-V3-01 contract once that WP is merged.
- TKN-V3-01 (WP-005) is a gating keystone for CMD-V3-01, OPT-V3-01, IMG-V3-01 — dispatch those only after WP-005 merges.
- Concurrency cap: 8 heavy workers in flight at any time.
- WP-004 and WP-005 are both XL/L effort — they will be in flight simultaneously. Watch for `engine.py` conflicts if both are active; they touch different sub-areas.

Good — 6 of 8 WPs have real content. Two issues to flag upfront:
- **WP-002 (EDT)**: branch exists but 0 commits — **not implemented**
- **WP-007 (SUN)**: commit `ab34882d` landed on the phasing branch (`feat/v3-krn-v3-01-phasing`) instead of its own branch, but the work is complete and solid

All other 6 WPs look well-scoped with tests. Several branches have extra seed/viewport commits mixed in from parallel sessions — I'll cherry-pick just the WP commits rather than doing full branch merges to keep main clean.

Let me check the base state and run validation before touching main.

=== WP-004 commands.py diff (first 40 lines) ===
commit 31cae0bb55dd11678ba19eb36d9d4bcd0ad20404
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 16:24:40 2026 +0200

    feat(kernel): KRN-V3-01 phasing primitive
    
    Introduces the Phase element kind and phasing fields across the BIM
    kernel — Python, TypeScript, and CLI layers — per spec/v3-prompts/wp-004.
    
    Added:
    - PhaseElem (kind: "phase", ord, name) to elements.py + TS index.ts
    - phase_created / phase_demolished fields on 16 physical element classes
    - phase_filter field (PhaseFilter literal) on PlanViewElem
    - 7 new Pydantic commands: CreatePhase, RenamePhase, ReorderPhase,
      DeletePhase, SetElementPhase, SetViewPhase, SetViewPhaseFilter
    - 7 match cases in engine.apply_inplace with ord-collision and
      reference-integrity guards
    - try_commit_bundle now catches ValueError/KeyError from apply_inplace
      and Pydantic ValidationError from coerce_command, returning (False,...)
    - 7 CLI handlers in cli.mjs (phase-create, phase-rename, phase-reorder,
      phase-delete, element-set-phase, view-set-phase, view-set-phase-filter)
    - 20 tests in app/tests/test_phasing_primitive.py (all pass)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: 46163be531b7

diff --git a/app/bim_ai/commands.py b/app/bim_ai/commands.py
index 27ce9a87..9ab814f3 100644
--- a/app/bim_ai/commands.py
+++ b/app/bim_ai/commands.py
@@ -13,6 +13,7 @@ from bim_ai.elements import (
     DormerRoofKind,
     DxfLineworkPrim,
     EvidenceRef,
+    PhaseFilter,
     PlanCategoryGraphicRow,
     PlanTagBadgeStyle,
     PlanTagTarget,
@@ -1048,6 +1049,46 @@ class MoveSurveyPointCmd(BaseModel):
     shared_elevation_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="sharedElevationMm")
 
 
+# --- SUN-V3-01: sun_settings commands -------------------------------------------
+
+
+class CreateSunSettingsCmd(BaseModel):
+    """SUN-V3-01: create the project-level sun settings singleton.
+
+    Rejects if one already exists (use UpdateSunSettings to modify).
+    """
+
+    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
+    type: Literal["createSunSettings"] = "createSunSettings"
+    id: str | None = None
+    latitude_deg: float = Field(default=48.13, alias="latitudeDeg")
+    longitude_deg: float = Field(default=11.58, alias="longitudeDeg")
+    date_iso: str = Field(default="2026-06-21", alias="dateIso")
+    time_of_day: dict = Field(
+        default_factory=lambda: {"hours": 14, "minutes": 30}, alias="timeOfDay"
+    )

=== WP-005 commands.py diff ===
commit 14c2fd6e283c137de5564862864ca89e933c1413
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 16:06:47 2026 +0200

    feat(tkn): tokenised kernel representation — encode/decode/validate/diff
    
    Introduces app/bim_ai/tkn/ (pure-function token layer) with:
    - encode: elements → deterministic TokenSequence
    - decode: TokenSequence × kernel_state → commands
    - validate: structural advisory checks (orphan host, out-of-range)
    - diff: structural delta between two TokenSequences
    
    TypeScript types (EntityToken, EnvelopeToken, TokenSequence,
    TokenSequenceDelta) added to packages/core. CLI bim-ai tokens
    encode|decode|diff registered per WP-005 API-V3-01. MoveElementCmd
    added to commands + engine handler. Round-trip wired into
    try_commit_bundle as a determinism gate. 24 acceptance tests green.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: ad96a624d3c0

index 27ce9a87..b22987a6 100644
--- a/app/bim_ai/commands.py
+++ b/app/bim_ai/commands.py
@@ -1545,6 +1545,15 @@ class CreateConstraintCmd(BaseModel):
     severity: Literal["warning", "error"] = "error"
 
 
+class MoveElementCmd(BaseModel):
+    """TKN-V3-01 — move a wall-hosted element (door/window) to a new tAlongHost position."""
+
+    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
+    type: Literal["moveElement"] = "moveElement"
+    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
+    t_along_host: float = Field(alias="tAlongHost", ge=0.0, le=1.0)
+
+
 Command = Annotated[
     CreateLevelCmd

commit 30c7e3cc29670f39138784fe27209676b0f31397
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Fri May 8 16:11:32 2026 +0200

    feat(kernel): KRN-V3-06 — plan region cut-plane override
    
    Implements WP-006: sub-rectangle in a plan view with a different
    cut-plane height than the parent view.
    
    Python engine:
    - UpdatePlanRegionCmd + DeletePlanRegionCmd (commands.py)
    - Dispatch cases in engine.py (update supports name, outline, cut-plane;
      delete validates element kind before removal)
    
    TypeScript:
    - extractPlanRegionOverlays + pointInPolygonMm in planProjection.ts
    - PlanRegionOverlay type export
    - plan-region in PlanTool (storeTypes.ts), ToolId + registry entry
      (toolRegistry.ts), KNOWN_PLAN_TOOLS + LegacyPlanTool (workspaceUtils.ts)
    - PlanCanvas: import, Draft union variant, KRN-V3-06 dashed boundary
      rendering via --draft-witness CSS token, two-click tool handler
    - InspectorPlanRegionEditor component in InspectorContent.tsx
    
    CLI:
    - plan-region create/update/delete subcommands in cli.mjs
    
    Tests:
    - 11 Python engine tests (test_plan_region_commands.py)
    - 8 TypeScript unit tests (planProjection.planRegion.test.ts)
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Entire-Checkpoint: a3d14173ba43

+++ b/app/bim_ai/commands.py
@@ -684,6 +684,20 @@ class CreatePlanRegionCmd(BaseModel):
     outline_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="outlineMm")
     cut_plane_offset_mm: float = Field(alias="cutPlaneOffsetMm", default=-500)

    if (cmd === 'tokens') {
      const sub = argv[0];
      if (sub === 'encode') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/models/${modelId}/tokens/encode`, {
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        });
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`tokens encode: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
        console.log(JSON.stringify(await res.json(), null, 2));
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'decode') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const filePath = argv[1];
        let seqJson;
        if (!filePath || filePath === '-') {
          const chunks = [];
          for await (const chunk of process.stdin) chunks.push(chunk);
          seqJson = JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(chunks).toString());
        } else {
          const { readFileSync } = await import('fs');
          seqJson = JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
        }
        const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/models/${modelId}/tokens/decode`, {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
          body: JSON.stringify({ sequence: seqJson }),
        });
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`tokens decode: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
        console.log(JSON.stringify(await res.json(), null, 2));
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'diff') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const aPath = argv.find((_, i) => argv[i - 1] === '--a');
        const bPath = argv.find((_, i) => argv[i - 1] === '--b');
        if (!aPath || !bPath) {
          console.error('Usage: tokens diff --a <path> --b <path>');
          process.exit(1);
        }
        const { readFileSync } = await import('fs');
        const seqA = JSON.parse(readFileSync(aPath, 'utf8'));
        const seqB = JSON.parse(readFileSync(bPath, 'utf8'));
        const res = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/models/${modelId}/tokens/diff`, {
          method: 'POST',
          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
          body: JSON.stringify({ sequenceA: seqA, sequenceB: seqB }),
        });
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`tokens diff: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
        console.log(JSON.stringify(await res.json(), null, 2));
        return;
      }
      console.error(`Unknown tokens subcommand: ${sub ?? '(none)'}. Use encode, decode, or diff.`);
=======
    if (cmd === 'plan-region') {
      const sub = argv[0];
      if (sub === 'create') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        let levelId, cutPlaneOffsetMm, name, coords;
        const rest = argv.slice(1);
        for (let i = 0; i < rest.length; i++) {
          if (rest[i] === '--level') levelId = rest[++i];
          else if (rest[i] === '--cut') cutPlaneOffsetMm = Number(rest[++i]);
          else if (rest[i] === '--name') name = rest[++i];
          else coords = rest.slice(i);
        }
        if (!levelId || !coords || coords.length < 4) {
          console.error('plan-region create: --level <id> x0 y0 x1 y1 required');
          process.exit(1);
        }
        const [x0, y0, x1, y1] = coords.map(Number);
        const cmd = {
          type: 'createPlanRegion',
          levelId,
          outlineMm: [
            { xMm: x0, yMm: y0 },
            { xMm: x1, yMm: y0 },
            { xMm: x1, yMm: y1 },
            { xMm: x0, yMm: y1 },
          ],
          ...(cutPlaneOffsetMm !== undefined ? { cutPlaneOffsetMm } : {}),
          ...(name !== undefined ? { name } : {}),
        };
        await commit(modelId, cmd);
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'update') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const id = argv[1];
        if (!id) { console.error('plan-region update: <id> required'); process.exit(1); }
        const rest = argv.slice(2);
        const updates = { type: 'updatePlanRegion', id };
        for (let i = 0; i < rest.length; i++) {
          if (rest[i] === '--cut') updates.cutPlaneOffsetMm = Number(rest[++i]);
          else if (rest[i] === '--name') updates.name = rest[++i];
        }
        await commit(modelId, updates);
        return;
      }
      if (sub === 'delete') {
        if (!modelId) usage();
        const id = argv[1];
        if (!id) { console.error('plan-region delete: <id> required'); process.exit(1); }
        await commit(modelId, { type: 'deletePlanRegion', id });
        return;
      }
      console.error(`Unknown plan-region subcommand: ${sub ?? '(none)'}. Use create, update, or delete.`);
